Max Ernst

1 work in the gallery.
Click any image to enlarge. Use ← → or close with Esc.
The Elephant Celebes
by Max Ernst
Max Ernst · 1921 · Tate Modern
A giant metallic elephant based on a photograph of a Sudanese corn bin looms over a headless mannequin beside a mysterious fish-stalk. Ernst's breakthrough Surrealist work, painted before André Breton had even coined the term.
←Forbes-Robertson, Eric Stuven, Ernst→
